title: UXMLREADER
text: UXMLREADER UXMLREADER is an internal Uniface component that enables you to parse XML documents stored in files, fields, or variables. The UXMLREADER acts as an intermediary between the SAX parser and a SAX event handler. To use the UXMLREADER you need to implement a component that handles SAX callback operations. Operations PARSE —parses a specified XML stream. PARSEFILE —parses an XML document in the specified file. SETHANDLER —specifies the component instance to serve as the SAX handler. Related Links Creating a SAX Event Handler Before starting to parse XML documents using the UXMLREADER component, you need to implement the operations that handle parser events, such as starting and ending the document and elements, and error handling. SAX Callback Operations SAX callback operations are APIs that can be implemented on a user-defined SAX event handler. They enable you to use the parser information to manipulate and analyze XML data, and handle any errors that may be encountered. Reading and Parsing Customized XML You can parse XML documents stored in files, fields, or variables using the UXMLREADER component API. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
